title: Combustion and Flame 144 (2006) 225–236
abstract:
The thermochemical states of three swirling CH4/air diffusion ﬂames, stabilized in a gas turbine model com-
bustor, were investigated using laser Raman scattering. The ﬂames were operated at different thermal powers
and air/fuel ratios and exhibited different ﬂame behavior with respect to ﬂame instabilities. They had previously
been characterized with respect to their ﬂame structures, velocity ﬁelds, and mean values of temperature, major
species concentrations, and mixture fraction. The single-p
